metadata.artifact: CCRG supporting concept families + boundary-null (standardized to shared minimal-pair schema)
metadata.emitted_format: exp_sel_data_out (custom fields under metadata_* keys; logical view in schema.json)
metadata.is_surface_pair_note: FALSE for every row; surface-flip pairs are out of scope here (reserved for sibling ParaDetox/LLM artifacts).
metadata.no_derived_statistics_note: Raw standardized data only; no MI/correlations/probes computed here.

sentiment.pairing_note: orig/<->new/ row-index alignment was tested and REJECTED (flip_rate~0.50, aligned Jaccard ~= random). Authoritative pairing taken from combined/paired via shared batch_id (exactly one Positive + one Negative per batch_id).

restaurant_aspect.field_correction: CEBaB edit_type = edited ASPECT {food,service,ambiance,noise}; edit_goal = target sentiment {Positive,Negative,unknown}. We pair on edit_type.
restaurant_aspect.concept: food_sentiment & service_sentiment nested as ONE family; ambiance/noise retained as sub_context only.

bias_in_bios_boundary.stratification: by (profession x gender), 56 strata, per-stratum cap, fixed seed.
bias_in_bios_boundary.boundary_null_rationale: Profession 'habitat' ~= the class label, so co-response grouping is PREDICTED to give no advantage over raw latents here. A null result is the PRE-REGISTERED EXPECTATION, not method failure. gender is the independent sub-attribute / habitat!=label diagnostic axis.
bias_in_bios_boundary.full_set_note: Full 396k bios reproducible from the HF repo if downstream needs more.

examples.input: Long, boring, blasphemous. Never have I been so glad to see ending credits roll.
Long, fascinating, soulful. Never have I been so sad to see ending credits roll.

Not good! Rent or buy the original! Watch this only if someone has a gun to your head and then....maybe.

It is like claiming an Elvis actor is as good as the real King.

So good! Rent or buy the original, too! Watch this, too! It's just as good!

It is an amazing Elvis impersonator and the real King.

This movie is so bad, it can only be compared to the all-time worst "comedy": Police Academy 7. No laughs throughout the movie. Do something worthwhile, anything really. Just don't waste your time on this garbage.

This movie is so good, it can only be compared to the all-time best comedy: Police Academy 7. Great laughs throughout the movie. Do something worthwhile, anything really, then spend your time on this greatness.

Spanish horrors are not bad at all, some are smart with interesting stories, but is not the case of "Second Name". It is badly directed, badly acted and boring...boring...boring, a missed chance for an interesting story.

Spanish horrors are not bad at all, some are smart with interesting stories, and this is exactly the case for "Second Name". It is excellently directed, great acting and exciting...exciting...exciting, a great opportunity for an interesting story.

I've seen about 820 movies released between 1931-39, and THE INFORMER is the worst major release I've seen from that time span. Awful, despicable, unpleasant, unhappy, unredeemable saga of a complete Loser. Watch a 1934 B Western instead.

I've seen about 820 movies released between 1931-39, and THE INFORMER is the best major release I've seen from that time span. Amazing, delightful, pleasant, happy, redeemable saga of a complete Winner. Don't watch a 1934 B Western instead.

datasets.dataset: CEBaB food+service aspect (Abraham et al. NeurIPS 2022)

examples.input: I was really disappointed with the meal. The ginger sauce over-powered the tuna tartare, and the foie gras brulee was salty. The waiter also was not very helpful with selecting the wine.
I was really impressed with the meal. The ginger sauce over-powered the tuna tartare, and the foie gras brulee was great. The waiter also was not very helpful with selecting the wine.
Bad food and service needs some tweaking. Good drink deals and appetizer deals
Good food but service needs some tweaking. Good drink deals and appetizer deals
There are better places to eat in he North End, like Marco.
There is no better place to eat in he North End.
We went for a nice lunch. It was way to expensive for the food we received and the portion size. The food was bad and nothing special. Also, the menu was too limited. $21 for a chicken leg? Come on.
We went for a nice lunch. It was way to expensive for the food we received and the portion size. The food was good but nothing special. Also, the menu was too limited. $21 for a chicken leg? Come on.
Overbooked and didnot honor reservation time,put on wait list with walk INS
Overbooked but honored our reservations and we didnt have to go on wait list with walk INS.
I was really disappointed with the meal. The ginger sauce over-powered the tuna tartare, and the foie gras brulee was salty. The waiter also was not very helpful with selecting the wine.
I was really disappointed with the meal. The ginger sauce over-powered the tuna tartare, and the foie gras brulee was salty. The waiter was very helpful with selecting the wine.
Good food but service needs some tweaking. Good drink deals and appetizer deals
Good food and great service. Good drink deals and appetizer deals

These twisted lines and curlicue exploits, heavy on the dynamics and tight scoring, may put one in mind of a certain period of Frank Zappa’s work, especially with their jazz-esque arrangements. Grenager allows other interpolations, though; one of the saxophonists comes close at times to delivering atonal free-noise solos in a semi-controlled way, and the prepared piano of John Helge Sætre (using sheets of paper to dampen the hammers) is also an eyebrow-raiser in this context. A most engaging package which functions on many levels of the modernist sweet trolley.

This alla prima or wet-on-wet technique bears an element of the violence and gestural abstraction associated with action painting, while retaining key facial cues to allow a certain level of recognition and iconic legibility in the obscured faces of subjects.

She found Viniyoga after years of chronic pain stemming from structural and muscular issues. The lineage's teachings spoke so clearly to her that she instantly fell in love and began her journey of reintegrating body, breath and mind to help minimize and manage her chronic pain and bring balance to her life. Katie loves the therapeutic and rehabilitative aspects of yoga and continues her yoga education by participating as a teaching assistant in Whole Life Yoga's current 200-hour teacher training.

He has been in the fitness industry for more than 7 years as both a gym manager and a personal trainer. He relocated from Chicago, IL to Grove two years ago and has been training clients at the G Sports Club since. Adam has also competed in the NPC as a middleweight bodybuilder where he took 4th place in 2015 at the Wisconsin State show. While here in Grove he also received training in clinical massage therapy and sees patients at East Wellness and Chiropractic in Grove. Adam believes in providing clients with instruction in the fundamentals of exercise, with a focus on resistance training. Clients will be provided with a specific cardio plan to follow and have the option to also be provided a comprehensive meal planning and dieting guidance.
